hero.headline: Visualise complex relationships in text
hero.subheading: An interactive demo exploring how network graphs can surface hidden structure in unstructured text. Select files, choose a view mode, and explore connections.

about.label: About
about.heading: From raw text to relational structure
about.body: This project investigates methods for extracting meaningful entity relationships from natural language text and representing them as interactive network graphs — enabling faster, more intuitive analysis of large document sets.

details.title: Approach
details.body: Named entity recognition paired with co-occurrence analysis to build weighted edge graphs from arbitrary text corpora.

details.title: Goal
details.body: Enable researchers to visually compare relationship structures across multiple documents simultaneously.

details.title: Stage
details.body: Prototype — demonstrating core interaction patterns with synthetic datasets.

demo.label: Interactive demo
demo.heading: Network graph explorer
demo.subheading: Select files and a mode. Scroll to zoom, drag canvas to pan, drag nodes to reposition.

cards.title: Structural divergence
cards.body: Documents from different domains produce visually distinct graph topologies, even at similar word counts.

cards.title: Hub entities
cards.body: A small number of entities act as hubs across multiple documents, revealing cross-document thematic links.

cards.title: Edge density as signal
cards.body: Higher edge density correlates with more tightly scoped, technical writing styles versus broad narrative text.

footer.right: Add datasets in the data/ folder
